notes.note: The proceeding is a divinatio, the preliminary hearing in which a court decided which of several rival accusers should conduct a prosecution; here the contest is to lead the case against Gabinius before the praetor Cato's court.

notes.anchor: he was going to demand a triumph
notes.note: A triumph was the formal victory parade voted by the Senate to a returning general; Gabinius's claim to one for his Syrian and Egyptian campaigns was both presumptuous and politically inflammatory, given that he had restored Ptolemy without authorization.

The publicani were the companies of tax-farmers who held the state revenue contracts; Gabinius had cheated them of their Syrian profits, so they testified against him in the Senate and joined its uproar.
When no consuls could be elected, patrician senators held office in turn as interrex (five days each) until valid elections could be held; the deadlock of 54 BC threatened exactly this.

The lex curiata de imperio was the formal grant of military and civil authority that the curiate assembly conferred on an incoming magistrate; Appius's claim to govern his province without it was a notorious irregularity.
